title: cheirolophus canariensis var. subexpinnata
intro: Cheirolophus canariensis var. subexpinnata, also known as Canary Islands cheirolophus or subexpinnate cheirolophus, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Canary Islands, specifically on the island of La Palma. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant effects. The health benefits of Cheirolophus canariensis var. subexpinnata include relief from respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to soothe skin irritations and wounds. It can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and potential side effects.
